description.en: A laid-back southern Thai city renowned for its roasted pork, hidden emerald caves, and pristine islands. Trang offers an authentic slice of southern Thai life with a thriving food culture and access to stunning Andaman Sea islands.
description.nl: Een ontspannen Zuid-Thaise stad beroemd om zijn geroosterd varkensvlees, verborgen smaragdgroene grotten en ongerepte eilanden. Trang biedt een authentiek stukje Zuid-Thais leven met een bloeiende eetcultuur en toegang tot prachtige eilanden in de Andamanzee.

enhanced_description:
I nearly missed Trang entirely. My original plan was to take the ferry from Krabi straight to Koh Lanta, but a chance conversation with a Thai food writer on the bus changed everything. 'You cannot go to the Andaman coast without eating in Trang,' she said, her eyes widening as if I had suggested skipping breakfast forever. 'The moo yang — the roasted pork — you will dream about it.' She was right. Trang turned out to be one of the great food cities of Thailand, a place where the Sino-Thai culinary tradition has produced a breakfast culture unlike anywhere else in the country, and where the surrounding islands rival anything the more famous destinations can offer.

The first morning I walked down Ratchadamnoen Road at 6 AM and found the city already humming. Trang's signature breakfast ritual revolves around moo yang — whole pigs slow-roasted over charcoal until the skin crackles and the meat melts — served alongside fresh dim sum and strong local coffee. I sat at Ko Pi, one of the most famous breakfast spots on Phraram VI Road, and ordered a plate of roast pork with crispy skin, a steaming basket of siu mai dumplings, and a glass of Trang-style filtered coffee so thick and aromatic it could wake the dead. The pork was extraordinary — sweet, smoky, the fat rendered to a glassy crunch, the meat tender and deeply flavored. Around me, multigenerational families shared tables, grandmothers ordered for everyone, children dipped dumplings in chili vinegar, and the whole scene felt like witnessing a city-wide family breakfast. This is the Chinese-Thai heritage of Trang at its most delicious.

The islands off Trang's coast are where the landscape takes your breath away. I hired a longtail boat from Pak Meng pier and headed to Koh Muk, a small island with a handful of guesthouses and one of the most remarkable natural wonders I have ever seen: Tham Morakot, the Emerald Cave. To reach it, you swim through an 80-meter sea cave in near-total darkness — the water cold against your chest, the cave roof inches above your head, the sound of your own breathing amplified in the blackness — and then suddenly emerge into a hidden inland beach, a perfect crescent of white sand enclosed by towering limestone cliffs open to the sky. The water in the cave entrance glows an impossible emerald green where sunlight penetrates the rock. I sat on that secret beach for an hour, drying in the sun, utterly alone, feeling like I had discovered something out of a novel.

Back in town, the Trang night market on Sathani Road is a quieter affair than Bangkok's famous markets, but the food is arguably better. I worked my way through plates of khanom jeen with yellow curry, grilled squid on sticks, coconut pancakes called khanom krok, and a local specialty of deep-fried fish cakes so crisp and fragrant they disappeared in seconds. An older woman running a dessert stall handed me a piece of khanom piak poon — a translucent jelly noodle in sweet coconut milk — and when I told her it was delicious, she beamed and gave me a second bowl for free. Trang's night market is not a tourist attraction; it is simply where Trang eats dinner, and being welcomed into that nightly ritual felt like a privilege.

The coastline south of Trang stretches toward the Malaysian border through some of the least-developed beach territory in the Andaman region. I spent a day at Hat Chao Mai National Park, where long stretches of casuarina-lined beach meet clear water and the only other visitors were a family of sea eagles circling overhead. The Trang Islands — Koh Kradan, Koh Libong, Koh Ngai — are each distinct: Kradan has what many consider the most beautiful beach in Thailand (a bold claim, but standing on its eastern shore watching the sun set behind Koh Muk, I was inclined to agree), Libong is famous for its dugong population, and Ngai offers snorkeling straight off the beach in coral gardens teeming with parrotfish and clownfish. Trang gives you all of this without the crowds, without the full-moon-party infrastructure, without the sense that paradise has been optimized for Instagram. It is Thailand's Andaman coast the way it used to be, and for how much longer it will stay that way, I honestly cannot say.

description.en: Often called the most beautiful beach in Thailand, this small island's eastern shore has powdery white sand meeting crystal-clear water over a coral reef. No roads, no ATMs, just pristine beach perfection with a handful of small resorts.
description.nl: Vaak het mooiste strand van Thailand genoemd, heeft de oostkust van dit kleine eiland poederachtig wit zand dat kristalhelder water ontmoet boven een koraalrif. Geen wegen, geen geldautomaten, alleen puur strandperfectie met een handvol kleine resorts.

description.en: A legendary Trang breakfast institution on Phraram VI Road serving perfect roasted pork, handmade dim sum, and strong filtered coffee since the 1940s. Arrive before 7 AM to secure a seat among the local regulars.
description.nl: Een legendarisch Trang ontbijtinstituut aan de Phraram VI Road dat perfecte gebraden varkensvlees, handgemaakte dim sum en sterke filterkoffie serveert sinds de jaren 1940. Kom voor 7 uur aan om een stoel te bemachtigen tussen de vaste locale klanten.

description.en: One of the last places in Thailand to spot wild dugongs grazing on seagrass beds. The island's east coast is a protected marine area where these gentle sea mammals can be observed from boats in the early morning.
description.nl: Een van de laatste plaatsen in Thailand om wilde doejongs te zien grazen op zeegrasgebieden. De oostkust van het eiland is een beschermd marien gebied waar deze vriendelijke zeezoogdieren 's ochtends vroeg vanuit boten kunnen worden geobserveerd.

description.en: The tree-lined plaza around the monument to Trang's beloved governor features old colonial-era buildings, morning exercise groups, and a lively food stall area. A perfect spot to observe daily Trang life with a coffee in hand.
description.nl: Het met bomen omzoomde plein rond het monument van Trang's geliefde gouverneur heeft oude koloniale gebouwen, ochtendgymnastiekgroepen en een levendig eetkraampjesgebied. Een perfecte plek om het dagelijks leven van Trang te observeren met een koffie in de hand.

description.en: A series of limestone cascades in a forest park just 40 minutes from Trang town. The natural pools are popular swimming spots for locals on weekends, with almost no foreign visitors and a peaceful jungle atmosphere.
description.nl: Een reeks kalksteencascades in een bospark op slechts 40 minuten van de stad Trang. De natuurlijke zwembaden zijn populaire zwemplekken voor locals in het weekend, met bijna geen buitenlandse bezoekers en een vredige jungle-sfeer.

description.en: The most iconic breakfast spot in Trang, famous for its moo yang (roasted pork), homemade dim sum, and strong Trang-style coffee. A multi-generational family institution where locals start every morning.
description.nl: De meest iconische ontbijtplek in Trang, beroemd om zijn moo yang (geroosterd varkensvlees), huisgemaakte dim sum en sterke Trang-stijl koffie. Een meergeneratie familiebedrijf waar locals elke ochtend beginnen.

description.en: A beloved late-night rice porridge shop on Huay Yod Road. Their khao tom with fresh seafood, century egg, and minced pork is the go-to meal for Trang's night owls. Simple, warming, and open until the early hours.
description.nl: Een geliefd laat-avond rijstpapwinkel aan de Huay Yod Road. Hun khao tom met verse zeevruchten, eeuwenoud ei en gehakt varkensvlees is de favoriete maaltijd voor Trang's nachtbrakers. Simpel, verwarmend en open tot in de vroege uurtjes.

description.en: Trang's best roti shop serving crispy, flaky flatbread with curry or sweet condensed milk. The Muslim-Thai community has perfected this dish over generations. Their mataba (stuffed roti) with chicken curry is exceptional.
description.nl: Trang's beste roti-winkel serveert knapperig, bladerdeegachtig platbrood met curry of zoete gecondenseerde melk. De moslim-Thaise gemeenschap heeft dit gerecht over generaties geperfectioneerd. Hun mataba (gevulde roti) met kipcurry is uitzonderlijk.

description.en: The evening food stalls along the old train station road serve everything from grilled squid and fish cakes to coconut pancakes and jelly noodle desserts. An authentic local dining experience with rock-bottom prices.
description.nl: De avondvoedselkramen langs de oude treinstation weg serveren alles van gegrilde inktvis en viskoekjes tot kokospannenkoekjes en geleinoedel-desserts. Een authentieke lokale eetervaring met bodemprijzen.

description.en: A classic Trang dim sum house where the Chinese-Thai culinary tradition shines. Steamed dumplings, barbecue pork buns, and custard tarts arrive in bamboo steamers alongside pots of jasmine tea. Best visited before 9 AM.
description.nl: Een klassiek Trang dim sum huis waar de Chinese-Thaise culinaire traditie schittert. Gestoomde dumplings, barbecue varkensbroodjes en custardtaartjes komen in bamboestomers samen met kannetjes jasmijnthee. Het beste te bezoeken voor 9 uur.

description.en: Trang's largest and most well-known hotel, centrally located with comfortable rooms, a pool, and a popular breakfast buffet. A reliable base for exploring the city and booking island day trips.
description.nl: Trang's grootste en bekendste hotel, centraal gelegen met comfortabele kamers, een zwembad en een populair ontbijtbuffet. Een betrouwbare uitvalsbasis om de stad te verkennen en eilanddagtrips te boeken.

description.en: A stunning beachfront resort on the coast between Trang and Krabi. Luxurious rooms overlooking the Andaman Sea, infinity pools, spa, and direct access to island-hopping tours. The best luxury option in the Trang area.
description.nl: Een prachtig strandresort aan de kust tussen Trang en Krabi. Luxe kamers met uitzicht op de Andamanzee, infinity pools, spa en directe toegang tot eilandhopping-tours. De beste luxe optie in het Trang gebied.

description.en: A clean and friendly budget hotel in the city center, within walking distance of the night market and breakfast streets. Simple rooms with air conditioning and hot water at backpacker-friendly prices.
description.nl: Een schoon en vriendelijk budgethotel in het stadscentrum, op loopafstand van de nachtmarkt en ontbijtstraten. Eenvoudige kamers met airconditioning en warm water voor backpacker-vriendelijke prijzen.

description.en: A beachfront resort on Koh Muk island, perfectly positioned for visiting the Emerald Cave. Bungalows sit right on the sand with views across to the mainland mountains. The most atmospheric island stay in the Trang archipelago.
description.nl: Een strandresort op het eiland Koh Muk, perfect gelegen voor een bezoek aan de Smaragd Grot. Bungalows staan direct op het zand met uitzicht op de bergen van het vasteland. Het meest sfeervolle eilandverblijf in de Trang archipel.

description.en: A small, social hostel popular with backpackers exploring the Trang islands. Helpful staff organize group tours to the Emerald Cave and Koh Kradan, making it easy to meet travel companions.
description.nl: Een klein, gezellig hostel populair bij backpackers die de Trang-eilanden verkennen. Behulpzaam personeel organiseert groepstours naar de Smaragd Grot en Koh Kradan, waardoor het makkelijk is om reisgenoten te ontmoeten.

content.en: Trang's breakfast culture is legendary. Wake up early and head to Phraram VI Road for moo yang (roasted pork), dim sum, and strong local coffee. This is a once-in-Thailand experience that rivals any meal in Bangkok. Shops close by 10 AM.
content.nl: De ontbijtcultuur van Trang is legendarisch. Sta vroeg op en ga naar Phraram VI Road voor moo yang (geroosterd varkensvlees), dim sum en sterke lokale koffie. Dit is een unieke ervaring in Thailand die elke maaltijd in Bangkok evenaart. Winkels sluiten om 10 uur.

content.en: Book island tours from Pak Meng pier (30 min from Trang town). A four-island tour covering Koh Muk, Koh Kradan, Koh Ngai, and Tham Morakot costs 800-1,500 THB. For a deeper experience, stay overnight on Koh Muk or Koh Kradan.
content.nl: Boek eilandtours vanaf Pak Meng pier (30 min van Trang stad). Een vier-eilandentour met Koh Muk, Koh Kradan, Koh Ngai en Tham Morakot kost 800-1.500 THB. Voor een diepere ervaring, overnacht op Koh Muk of Koh Kradan.

content.en: Visit Tham Morakot (Emerald Cave) during low tide for the easiest swim through the 80-meter cave. Bring a waterproof bag for your camera. The cave can be crowded midday — arrive early or late afternoon for a more magical experience.
content.nl: Bezoek Tham Morakot (Smaragd Grot) tijdens eb voor de makkelijkste zwemtocht door de 80 meter lange grot. Neem een waterdichte tas mee voor je camera. De grot kan druk zijn rond het middaguur — kom vroeg of laat in de middag voor een magischere ervaring.

content.en: The city center is walkable, but you'll need a motorbike (200 THB/day) or tuk-tuk for beaches and piers. Minivans run from Trang to Pak Meng pier hourly. There's no Grab service — negotiate tuk-tuk prices before boarding.
content.nl: Het stadscentrum is bewandelbaar, maar je hebt een motor (200 THB/dag) of tuk-tuk nodig voor stranden en pieren. Minibusjes rijden elk uur van Trang naar Pak Meng pier. Er is geen Grab service — onderhandel tuk-tuk prijzen voordat je instapt.

content.en: Trang's islands are quieter and cheaper from May to October, though seas can be rough. The city itself is great year-round since the food scene never closes. Some island resorts close entirely in low season, so book ahead.
content.nl: Trang's eilanden zijn rustiger en goedkoper van mei tot oktober, hoewel de zee ruw kan zijn. De stad zelf is het hele jaar door geweldig omdat de eetscene nooit sluit. Sommige eilandresorts sluiten helemaal in het laagseizoen, dus boek vooruit.

best_time_to_visit.en: November to April offers the best weather for island hopping with calm seas and sunny skies. December to February is peak season. The city's food scene is excellent year-round. May to October brings rain and rougher seas, but lower prices and fewer tourists on the islands.
best_time_to_visit.nl: November tot april biedt het beste weer voor eilandhoppen met kalme zeeën en zonnige luchten. December tot februari is het hoogseizoen. De eetscene van de stad is het hele jaar door uitstekend. Mei tot oktober brengt regen en ruwere zeeën, maar lagere prijzen en minder toeristen op de eilanden.

overview: Trang is a laid-back southern Thai gem that remains refreshingly authentic and largely untouched by mass tourism. This coastal province has earned a stellar reputation among food lovers for its exceptional roasted pork (Moo Yang) and dim sum breakfast culture that rivals Bangkok's offerings. Beyond the culinary scene, Trang serves as the gateway to some of Thailand's most pristine islands and natural wonders, including the famous Emerald Cave with its hidden inland beach, making it an ideal base for both relaxation and adventure.
thingsToDo: The main draw for visitors is exploring Trang's island network and natural attractions. The Emerald Cave (Tham Morakot) on Koh Muk is the crown jewel—an 80-meter swim through a limestone cave leads to an emerald-green lagoon and a secret beach hidden within the island (entrance fee: 200 THB/$6). Koh Kradan offers pristine white sand beaches and excellent coral reef snorkeling with spectacular sunsets. For wildlife enthusiasts, Hat Chao Mai National Park provides opportunities to spot dugongs among mangrove forests, while Koh Libong is known for wild dugong watching in its seagrass beds. Pak Meng Beach, free to access, serves as the departure point for island tours and features fresh seafood restaurants along its long sandy shore. Closer to town, the Phraya Ratsadanupradit Monument sits in a tree-lined plaza near the morning market, offering a glimpse into local history and culture. Koh Ngai and Koh Kradan provide excellent beach-front snorkeling opportunities for those seeking immediate marine exploration.
whereToStay: Trang town offers a range of accommodation options scattered throughout different neighborhoods, though specific hotel data is limited. The area around Pak Meng Beach provides easy access to island tours and features several beachfront and near-beach properties with seafood dining options. Budget guesthouses cluster in the town center near the night market and transportation hub, offering basic but clean rooms at very affordable rates. Mid-range hotels typically provide air conditioning, hot water, and sometimes mini-fridges, positioned near main streets or in quieter residential areas. For visitors seeking island experiences, several resorts operate on Koh Kradan, Koh Ngai, and Koh Muk, ranging from basic bungalows to more upscale beachfront properties. The peak season (November to February) sees higher occupancy, so advance booking is recommended. Many accommodations can arrange island tours and activities directly from their properties.

whereToEat.name: EggE Egg Egg
whereToEat.description: Family-run restaurant famous for fresh oysters and crab omelette rice bowls. Open only for brunch 10am-1pm, located in a labyrinth-like neighborhood location and highly recommended as a must-do on your Trang itinerary.

whereToEat.name: 339 Huan Khao Soi
whereToEat.description: Serves Khao Soi that rivals Chiang Mai's best offerings. The owner adapted the northern specialty to southern tastes, adding more heat while maintaining authentic flavors without excessive sweetness.

whereToEat.name: Ko Jang
whereToEat.description: Hidden tea omakase in a backalley shed with an eccentric owner who operates on his own schedule. Serves some of Thailand's best fruit tea. The unpredictability of whether it's open is part of the charm.

whereToEat.name: Lion's Tale
whereToEat.description: Contemporary Thai restaurant run by Mook, known for creative takes on local favorites and contributing significantly to Trang's evolving food scene.

whereToEat.name: Trang Night Market
whereToEat.description: Authentic local atmosphere with abundant southern Thai street food options. This is where locals eat and offers budget-friendly dining with genuine flavors and minimal added sugar compared to other regions.

whereToEat.name: Pak Meng Beach Seafood Restaurants
whereToEat.description: Multiple seafood establishments line Pak Meng Beach, offering fresh catches with sea views. Perfect for lunch or dinner after island tours with prices reflecting the fresh daily catch.

topHotels.name: Trang City Hotel
topHotels.description: Central location near transportation and night market with air conditioning and standard amenities. Convenient for exploring local food scene and accessing island tour departures.

topHotels.name: Baan Trang Hotel
topHotels.description: Basic but clean guesthouse popular with backpackers. Simple rooms with fans or air conditioning, close to street food vendors and local markets.

topHotels.name: Trang Boutique Hotel
topHotels.description: Charming mid-range option with personalized service and tasteful design. Offers easy access to restaurants and local attractions with helpful staff for activity planning.

topHotels.name: Koh Kradan Resort
topHotels.description: Beachfront resort on pristine Koh Kradan island with snorkeling directly from the property. Offers a balance of comfort and island authenticity with sunset views over the Andaman.

topHotels.name: Koh Ngai Bungalows
topHotels.description: Basic beachfront bungalows on small Koh Ngai island. Ideal for budget travelers seeking island life with immediate access to snorkeling and minimal crowds.

topHotels.name: Mook Dee Bungalow
topHotels.description: Simple bungalows near Pak Meng Beach with easy access to island tour departures. Close to seafood restaurants and an authentic local beach experience.

topHotels.name: Trang Palace Hotel
topHotels.description: Established hotel with solid amenities including restaurant and tour desk. Central location for exploring the city's food culture and organizing day trips to islands.

topHotels.name: Koh Muk Sivalai Beach Resort
topHotels.description: Upscale beachfront resort with direct Emerald Cave access and water activities included. Features Thai-style architecture with modern comfort and sunset dining overlooking pristine beaches.

topHotels.name: Kradan Khaolak Resort
topHotels.description: Premium island resort with personalized service and curated island experiences. Offers yoga, wellness, and gourmet dining with exclusive access to the island's best snorkeling spots.

topHotels.name: Trang Riverside Boutique
topHotels.description: Contemporary boutique hotel with garden setting overlooking local life. Combines modern amenities with traditional Thai hospitality and provides excellent restaurant recommendations.

gettingAround: Trang town is small enough to explore on foot for most attractions within the city center. Tuk-tuks are the primary local transport and operate on fixed routes or can be hired for specific journeys; short trips within town typically cost 30-50 THB ($1-2). Songthaews (shared red taxis) run fixed routes between Trang town and Pak Meng Beach and other nearby areas, accommodating multiple passengers at very affordable rates. For island hopping, Pak Meng Beach is the main departure point for speedboats to Koh Kradan, Koh Muk, Koh Ngai, and Koh Libong; many hotels can arrange bookings or you can arrange directly at the beach. Motorbike rental is popular for exploring beaches and nearby attractions at approximately 150-250 THB ($5-8) per day. Grab (Thailand's ride-hailing app) operates in Trang for more convenient transportation with transparent pricing, though it's less prevalent than in major tourist cities.
bestTimeToVisit: Trang's best travel season runs from November to February, when the weather is dry and pleasant with temperatures around 25-30°C (77-86°F). This period offers the most stable conditions for island activities and beach time. March to May brings hot and humid weather with temperatures exceeding 35°C (95°F), making outdoor activities more challenging but fewer tourists. The southwest monsoon from May to October brings heavy rainfall and rough seas, making island tours less frequent and water activities riskier—many island resorts reduce operations during this period. Trang is slightly less affected by monsoons than some areas, but September and October are still the wettest months. For food-focused visits, any season works since the night markets and restaurants operate year-round. If combining island trips with food exploration, November through January provides the optimal balance of excellent weather and manageable crowds.

budget.description: Budget travelers can stay in basic guesthouses (15-30 USD/night), eat at night markets and street stalls (2-4 USD per meal), use tuk-tuks and songthaews for transport (1-2 USD per ride), and enjoy free or cheap attractions like Pak Meng Beach. Island day trips cost 10-15 USD with basic tour operators. Daily total: $25-40.

midrange.description: Mid-range travelers stay in comfortable hotels with amenities (45-80 USD/night), dine at quality local restaurants and some tourist spots (5-10 USD per meal), use Grab occasionally (2-4 USD per ride), and pay entrance fees for attractions like Emerald Cave (6 USD) and Hat Chao Mai (6 USD). Private island tours run 25-40 USD. Daily total: $60-100.

luxury.description: Luxury visitors stay at beachfront or island resorts (120-250 USD/night), dine at upscale restaurants and resorts (15-30 USD per meal), use private transportation or guides (50+ USD daily), and book exclusive tours and activities (60-100 USD per activity). Multi-day island packages and wellness experiences add premium costs. Daily total: $200-400.

safetyTips: Trang is generally a safe destination with low violent crime rates. The local population is friendly and welcoming to visitors. Standard precautions apply: avoid displaying expensive jewelry or electronics, secure valuables in hotel safes, and be cautious with purses and bags in crowded night markets. When visiting islands, follow safe boating practices—wear life jackets, check weather conditions before departure, and use reputable tour operators. Road safety is important; if renting a motorbike, wear helmets and drive defensively as local traffic can be chaotic. Avoid traveling alone at night in unfamiliar areas and stick to well-lit, populated streets. The rainy season (September-October) can bring rough seas and occasional flooding in low-lying areas—check conditions before island trips. Political demonstrations are rare but possible; avoid any large gatherings. Overall, Trang remains one of Thailand's safest and most peaceful destinations.

faq.question: How do I get to Trang from Phuket or Krabi?
faq.answer: From Phuket, it's approximately 3 hours by car (180 km) heading south through Phang Nga. From Krabi, it's about 2-2.5 hours (130 km) heading south. Most visitors rent a car or take a minivan/bus from these cities. The nearest airport is Trang Airport (TST) with limited domestic flights, or you can fly into Phuket or Krabi airports and drive/take transport to Trang.

faq.question: What is the Emerald Cave and how do I visit it?
faq.answer: The Emerald Cave (Tham Morakot) is on Koh Muk island. You swim approximately 80 meters through a limestone cave to emerge in an emerald-green lagoon with a hidden beach inside the island. Entry fee is 200 THB ($6). Boats depart from Pak Meng Beach; a full tour costs 10-15 USD. Tours typically operate 8am-3pm, and you'll need reasonable swimming ability and comfort in water.

faq.question: What is Trang famous for eating?
faq.answer: Trang is renowned throughout Thailand for roasted pork (Moo Yang), which is available everywhere. Dim sum is a famous breakfast tradition with numerous shops serving fresh offerings. Fresh seafood is abundant and affordable. The food here uses less sugar than northern Thailand and balances heat and flavor exceptionally well. The night market offers authentic southern Thai street food at budget prices.

faq.question: Is Trang good for island hopping?
faq.answer: Absolutely. Trang has easy access to pristine islands including Koh Kradan (excellent beaches and snorkeling), Koh Muk (home to Emerald Cave), Koh Ngai (small island with coral gardens), Koh Libong (dugong watching), and others. Most islands have minimal development, offering an authentic island experience. Island tours depart from Pak Meng Beach at 10-15 USD per person for day trips.

faq.question: When is the best time to visit Trang?
faq.answer: November to February is ideal with dry weather, temperatures 25-30°C (77-86°F), and calm seas for island activities. December-January are peak but crowded. Avoid May-October monsoon season when heavy rain and rough seas limit island access. March-April is very hot (35°C+) but has fewer tourists. Consider visiting September-October only if you don't mind rain and reduced boat services.

faq.question: How much does a night out at Trang Night Market cost?
faq.answer: Extremely affordable. You can eat several dishes—pad thai, skewered meats, noodle soup, fresh fruit—for 30-50 THB ($1-1.50) per item. A complete meal with multiple dishes costs 60-100 THB ($2-3). Soft drinks are 10-15 THB (30-50 cents). It's the most budget-friendly way to experience authentic southern Thai food.

faq.question: Do I need a boat trip to visit Koh Kradan?
faq.answer: Yes, Koh Kradan is an island and requires boat transportation from Pak Meng Beach. Standard longtail boat tours operate 8am-3pm with 2-3 departures daily when weather permits. Cost is approximately 10-15 USD per person for a day trip. The 30-40 minute boat ride is scenic and calm during high season (November-February). Several resorts on Koh Kradan also offer accommodation if you want to stay overnight.

practicalInfo.nearestAirport: Trang Airport (TST) - Limited domestic flights; alternatively Phuket International Airport (HKT) 3 hours away or Krabi International Airport (HKT) 2-2.5 hours away
practicalInfo.travelTimeFromAirport: From Trang Airport: 20 minutes to town center | From Phuket: 3 hours by car | From Krabi: 2-2.5 hours by car
